title: hudsonia ericoides subsp. tomentosa
intro: Hudsonia ericoides subsp. tomentosa, also known as Hudson's holly fern or woolly hudsonia, is a subspecies of the Hudsonia ericoides plant, a low-growing, shrubby evergreen native to the northeastern United States and southeastern Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. The health benefits of Hudsonia ericoides subsp. tomentosa are attributed to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support immune function.
